summary: Validated subscription pricing change ($49.99 MXN / $4.99 USD with 30-day trial) and VISIBLE_BUSINESS_FILTER. Frontend pages all show new pricing correctly ($49.99 MXN, $4.99 USD, 30 days trial). Backend has 3 critical/high bugs: (1) /me/subscribe returns 500 because db.stripe_config has stale price_id from old Stripe account; (2) /admin/config still returns subscription_price_mxn=39.0 because db.platform_config wasn't updated; (3) /featured and /search endpoints incorrectly include businesses with subscription_status='none'/null/missing - only canceled is filtered out. 6/10 backend tests pass; 4 fail confirming the bugs.

critical.issue: Returns 500 'No such price: price_1TRaVsC4OfX1G9SSBbba9d1R'. db.stripe_config has stale price_id pointing to a Price that doesn't exist in the current Stripe (sk_test_...) account. Result: businesses CANNOT subscribe at all. get_or_create_stripe_price() returns cached price_id without validating it exists in Stripe. Same risk applies to POST /api/auth/business/create-subscription (registration flow) which uses the same helper.
critical.fix_suggestion: Either (a) drop existing rows from db.stripe_config so they get recreated, e.g. `db.stripe_config.delete_many({})`, or (b) wrap the cached lookup in a Stripe.Price.retrieve() call and recreate on InvalidRequestError, or (c) add a startup migration that validates and refreshes.

minor.issue: Returns subscription_price_mxn=39.0 from db.platform_config. Default constant is 49.99 but DB row was set when admin manually saved 39 in the past. Admin dashboard will display $39.
minor.fix_suggestion: Update DB: `db.platform_config.update_one({'_id':'main'}, {'$set':{'subscription_price_mxn':49.99}})` or migrate on startup.

minor.issue: Both endpoints use a permissive $or filter that includes businesses with subscription_status in ['none', null, missing] in addition to ['active','trialing']. Per requirement (and the VISIBLE_BUSINESS_FILTER constant in models/enums.py), only active/trialing should be visible. Filter in businesses.py lines 576-584 (search) and line 765/776 (featured) should be replaced with the strict VISIBLE_BUSINESS_FILTER constant.
minor.fix_suggestion: Replace the inline `$or` blocks with `dict(VISIBLE_BUSINESS_FILTER)` (already imported on line 44 but unused).

frontend_pass: /for-business: shows $49.99 MXN, $4.99 USD, 'Despues de tus 30 dias gratis', 'PRIMER MES GRATIS' badge - no old $39/$199/$299
/business/settings tab Suscripcion: shows '$49.99 MXN / mes' + secondary '$4.99 USD en EE. UU.' - no $39

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix stale Stripe price cache. Either purge db.stripe_config (will be recreated on next call) or add validation in get_or_create_stripe_price() to retry if Stripe.Price.retrieve(price_id) raises InvalidRequestError. Without this, no business can subscribe (current request returns 500).
HIGH: Update db.platform_config.subscription_price_mxn from 39.0 to 49.99 (admin UI shows 39 even though codebase says 49.99).

HIGH: Apply VISIBLE_BUSINESS_FILTER strictly in /api/businesses/featured (lines 765, 776) and /api/businesses search (lines 576-584). Currently the $or block lets through subscription_status='none' / null / missing, which contradicts the spec ('only active/trialing should be visible'). The constant is already imported but unused.
Optionally hide BusinessRegisterPage step 5 verification was not interactively tested (would require creating a brand-new biz), but text content and constants are confirmed in code via search.
critical_code_review_comments: models/enums.py VISIBLE_BUSINESS_FILTER is correctly defined as strict ['active','trialing'] but routers/businesses.py rebuilds the filter inline with a permissive $or that includes 'none'/null/missing. This makes the constant misleading - either change the filter to be strict everywhere or relax the constant. Inconsistency is a maintenance risk.

core/stripe_config.py get_or_create_stripe_price() blindly trusts the cached price_id. If a Stripe key is rotated or test data wiped, the function returns a dead price_id and every subscribe call fails. Wrap in try/Stripe.Price.retrieve and fall through to creation on InvalidRequestError.
/api/admin/config endpoint returns DB values which can drift from code constants. After a price change, must run a migration; otherwise admin UI shows old number.
routers/businesses.py duplicates the same long $or block 3+ times (search, featured base_filter, featured more_filter). Extract VISIBLE_BUSINESS_FILTER and reuse.
test_credentials: Business: testbiz_dashboard@test.com / TestBiz123! (already has subscription_status='active'). Admin login skipped (TOTP) but /api/admin/config tested via the admin_token fixture which fetched TOTP through /app/scripts/get_admin_totp.py.
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up 2 transient businesses in fixture seed_invisible_biz: TEST_canceled_<uuid> (subscription_status=canceled) and TEST_none_<uuid> (subscription_status=none). Both deleted in fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the 3 backend bugs, rerun: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_subscription_pricing.py -v. Endpoints: /api/admin/config (NOT /platform-config). /api/businesses (root) is the search endpoint. Business login: POST /api/auth/business/login with {email,password} returns 'token' field. testbiz_dashboard already has subscription_status=active so /me/subscribe will short-circuit with 'Already subscribed' (400) - to retest /me/subscribe must use a biz with subscription_status='none' or temporarily clear stripe_subscription_id field.
rca_of_the_issue: BUG-1 (subscribe 500): db.stripe_config has price_id='price_1TRaVsC4OfX1G9SSBbba9d1R' which doesn't exist in current Stripe test account (likely from previous Stripe key rotation). Code returns cached id without verifying. BUG-2 (admin config 39): db.platform_config row was inserted/updated long ago with subscription_price_mxn=39.0. The admin endpoint reads from DB, not from code constants. BUG-3 (filter): The inline filter is permissive `$or: [active/trialing, none, null, missing]` whereas the spec and the imported VISIBLE_BUSINESS_FILTER are strict `$in: [active, trialing]`. Canceled is excluded only by accident (it's not in the OR list).
